title: INTERNAL Error: ConstantFilter constant cannot be NULL - use IsNullFilter instead
description:
### What happens?

The below test case triggered an internal error in DuckDB.

### To Reproduce

```sql
CREATE TABLE  t0(c1 INT);
SELECT * FROM t0 WHERE ((t0.c1 IS DISTINCT FROM NULL) OR (NOT NULL));
```

### OS:

Ubuntu 22.04

### DuckDB Version:

v1.1.4-dev3741 ab8c909857

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Suyang Zhong

### Affiliation:

NUS

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a source build

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1(c1 INT, c2 VARCHAR(255));
SELECT * FROM t1 WHERE (c1 IS NULL) OR (c2 IS NULL);
CREATE TABLE t2(c3 FLOAT, c4 BOOLEAN);
SELECT * FROM t2 WHERE (c3 IS NULL) OR (c4 IS FALSE);
key_bug_features: Using NULL in ConstantFilter instead of IsNullFilter
